$comment: MediaSuite listing for the OpenAI plugin directory (7-section form). Same schema as submission.config.json (NotesBridge). PENDING before submit: (1) a hosted demo VIDEO (info.demoRecordingUrl), (2) reviewer demo account creds + seeded data, (3) a Terms of Service page, (4) set OPENAI_APPS_CHALLENGE on the gateway when the form issues the token.

info.name: MediaSuite
info.subtitle: Your creator content studio
info.description: MediaSuite connects ChatGPT to your creator content workspace — your media library, AI-scored clips, scheduled posts, and cross-platform analytics. Ask what's performing and why, plan a week of short-form posts from one long video, draft and schedule posts across YouTube/Instagram/TikTok/Threads/Pinterest/Bluesky, tailor per-platform captions, generate a media kit or performance brief, search your transcripts by meaning, and trace which posts drove real revenue. Everything is grounded in your own data — the connector reads and writes only your content, authenticated per-user via OAuth 2.1 (PKCE + Dynamic Client Registration). Publishing goes direct to your connected platform accounts; no third-party reposting service.
info.developerIdentityNote: Substring-matched against the verified identity (Dupree Ops LLC).

domainVerification.note: The gateway serves /.well-known/openai-apps-challenge from the OPENAI_APPS_CHALLENGE env var (route is live, returns 404 until set). When the form issues the token: `RAILWAY_API_TOKEN=<acct> railway variables --service media-suite-gateway --set OPENAI_APPS_CHALLENGE=<token>` then redeploy, then click Verify Domain.

reviewerDemo.note: MediaSuite's OAuth consent page authenticates with a HANDLE + mcp_ token (not email/password). Reviewers: add the connector, authorize, and on the consent page enter handle 'demo' + the token. The demo user reads an isolated seeded namespace; no real data is exposed. Verified end-to-end 2026-07-18. Re-seed with `npm run seed:demo`.

prompts: Plan a week of shorts from my latest long video
How have my views trended over the last month?

testCases.expected: Returns the demo account's top posts by views (platform, views, likes, caption, permalink) — renders as the top-content cards once approved.
Topline (post count, total/avg views, avg engagement, date span) + per-format breakdown + caption topic clusters.
The demo account's media assets (id, filename, contentType, bytes, ingestedAt).

testCases.prompt: Which platforms am I connected to?
testCases.expected: Per-platform groups with publishReady + accounts (handle, followerCount, isPrimary).

testCases.prompt: Add a content idea: turn the podcast into three shorts
testCases.expected: ChatGPT confirms, then add_idea stores an open idea and returns it.

testCasesNote: Exactly 5 positive test cases; run against seeded demo data.

negativeTestCases.scenario: A different scheduling/posting product — MediaSuite is not Buffer/Hootsuite/Later and should not trigger for them.
negativeTestCases.prompt: Add this to my Buffer queue.

negativeTestCases.scenario: A general task unrelated to the user's own media/social content — MediaSuite only works with the creator's own workspace.
negativeTestCases.prompt: Summarize this PDF contract for me.

negativeTestCases.scenario: Generic how-to advice, not an action on the user's own data — MediaSuite answers from the creator's own analytics, not general tips.
negativeTestCases.prompt: What are some tips to grow on YouTube?

releaseNotes: Initial release. MediaSuite brings your creator content workspace into ChatGPT — media library, clips, scheduling across platforms, cross-platform analytics, media kits, transcript search, and revenue attribution. Grounded entirely in your own data, per-user OAuth 2.1. Read tools are read-only; write tools (draft, schedule, capture idea, export) are additive and confirmed by you.
